response.justification: Bogdan is technically strong across the required stack — TypeScript, Node.js, REST APIs, React, AWS, and Kubernetes — and his AI/MCP work at re:cinq directly maps to the LLM nice-to-have. However, this is a Junior/Mid-Level role and he is a Staff-level engineer with 15+ years of professional experience spanning embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, and platform architecture. The seniority mismatch is severe and raises serious concerns about retention, engagement, and compensation fit. His database background skews NoSQL (MongoDB), with PostgreSQL not featured prominently despite SQL listed in skills. Only consider if there is strong contextual reason for him to step into a junior-scoped role (e.g., deliberate career pivot, personal interest in the domain).
strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js proficiency backed by production use at multiple companies, directly satisfying the core technical requirement
LLM and agentic infrastructure experience (MCP frameworks at re:cinq) covers the most valuable nice-to-have on the list
Kubernetes and AWS CDK exposure aligns with cloud infrastructure nice-to-haves; Berlin-based removes relocation friction for the high on-site requirement
concerns: Grossly overqualified for an entry/mid-level role — Staff and Senior titles across 15+ years of experience create near-certain retention and engagement risk
PostgreSQL experience is not demonstrated; MongoDB dominates his data layer history, which is a gap for a stack that explicitly prefers relational databases
No NestJS exposure visible in the resume, and React usage is mentioned but never positioned as a primary strength, leaving backend-to-frontend balance uncertain
